title: The Zone of Interest
logline: The daily domestic life of Nazi commandant Rudolf Höss and his family living adjacent to Auschwitz concentration camp, where the horrors of the Holocaust unfold just beyond their garden wall.
antagonistOrConflict: The moral vacuum and banality of evil existing alongside industrial genocide

actBreaks.description: The family's idyllic domestic routine is disrupted when Hedwig's mother Linna arrives and begins to grasp the reality of their location.

validation.ruling: Hedwig shows Linna the top garden and reveals the camp wall where 'maybe Esther Silberman is over there,' fundamentally shifting from domestic bliss to acknowledging the proximity of victims, making the Holocaust complicity explicit and irreversible.

actBreaks.description: Rudolf receives orders transferring him away from Auschwitz, fundamentally disrupting the family's established life.

validation.ruling: Hans witnesses Rudolf shouting orders to drown a prisoner fighting over an apple, then watches a gardener scatter human ash as fertilizer - this scene forces the children's innocence to confront the systematic horror, representing the final collapse of any moral boundaries.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a deliberately circular structure that begins and ends with Rudolf in institutional corridors, creating a sense of inescapable complicity. The narrative avoids traditional dramatic beats, instead building tension through accumulating domestic normalcy against an unseen but ever-present backdrop of genocide. Glazer constructs meaning through juxtaposition rather than plot - the horror emerges from what we don't see rather than what we do. The structure mirrors the psychological compartmentalization of its characters, with family life proceeding in willful ignorance of adjacent atrocities.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately slow and observational, with 144 scenes averaging just over a page each, creating a mosaic of mundane moments. Key accelerations occur around Linna's visit (scenes 27-52) and Rudolf's transfer orders (scenes 105-133), but the script consistently returns to the steady rhythm of domestic routine, making the horror all the more chilling through its absence from the frame.

incitingIncident.description: Claus and Hans lie in their dark bunk beds with pocket torches as Claus inspects thirteen gold teeth, holding three up to the light while others lie on his bedsheet.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. BOYS’ BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #27 is just a pleasant birthday dinner that doesn't shatter the status quo. Scene #28 shows Claus inspecting thirteen gold teeth by torchlight in his bedroom - this revelation of the children's proximity to and casual interaction with the horrors of the Holocaust truly shatters any illusion of ordinary domestic life and establishes the core moral conflict.

midpoint.description: In thermal night vision, Polish girl Aleksandra gathers pears from trees while Rudolf's voice reads Hansel and Gretel off-screen.

validation.ruling: Aleksandra gathering pears in thermal night vision while Rudolf reads Hansel & Gretel off-screen creates a haunting juxtaposition that shifts the narrative perspective beyond the villa walls, marking a point of no return where the victims' humanity is finally acknowledged alongside the perpetrators' domestic fantasy.

climax.description: Rudolf locks his office door and walks down the corridor pumping his fists in self-congratulation with a growing smile and skip in his step, then suddenly clutches the bannister and vomits on the floor.

validation.ruling: Scene #133 shows Rudolf merely standing in a corridor after vomiting. Scene #132 captures the true climax - Rudolf walks down the corridor pumping his fists in celebration of his promotion, then suddenly clutches the bannister and vomits, representing the peak confrontation between his professional triumph and physical/moral revulsion.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRYSIDE, LAKESIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Höss family enjoys a peaceful picnic by a lake with Hedwig feeding baby Annagret while the children play and search for strawberries.

scenes.heading: EXT. SILVER BIRCH WOODLAND - DAY, LATER
scenes.summary: The Höss family walks together through a birch woodland glade, with Rudolf carrying the baby and the boys sharing the hamper weight.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Two 1940s cars wind their way along a forest road and turn onto a country road.

scenes.heading: EXT. HÖSS’ CAR, COUNTRY ROAD - DUSK TO NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rudolf drives through the night as children play-sing in the back seat, his headlights illuminating the rushing road and towering pine trees.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOOKING AT HÖSS VILLA - NIGHT, L ATER
scenes.summary: Rudolf systematically turns off lights throughout the two-story villa, moving from downstairs to upstairs as the lit windows go dark one by one.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rudolf and Hedwig lie in separate beds as Hedwig turns off her lamp and looks at her husband, who lies contentedly with hands folded on his chest.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL, STAIRWELL HÖSS VILLA - DAY, NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Rudolf is blindfolded and carefully guided down the stairs by his sons Claus and Hans, warning him not to be late.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The boys lead blindfolded Rudolf in his SS officer uniform to the back garden where the family surprises him with a wooden canoe for his birthday.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOP GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Polish prisoner-gardener Bronek pushes a wheelbarrow along the villa's garden path parallel to the concrete wall topped with barbed wire that separates the villa from Auschwitz buildings.
scenes.turningPointNote: First direct visual connection between the family home and Auschwitz camp

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bronek delivers bags and packages from his wheelbarrow to German housekeeper Sophie, then hands Hedwig a large sack and paper package.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sophie carries supplies to the pantry while Hedwig takes the sack and package from Bronek, then calls for Polish housemaid Marta to take the sack upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Hedwig opens the sack to reveal an exquisite brown fur coat, tries it on in front of the mirror, and finds a tube of lipstick in the pocket.
scenes.turningPointNote: Discovery of personal items from victims reveals the source of the Höss family's luxuries

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRS, HÖSS VILLA - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hedwig hands the fur coat to Aniela on the stairs, instructing her to mend the lining carefully.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig tests the lipstick from the coat pocket on her hand and lips, then closes it in a drawer and wipes the lipstick away.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAMP ENTRANCE BY HÖSS GARDEN PATH - DAY
scenes.summary: Rudolf leads SS officer Bischoff and civilian engineers Sander and Prüfer through the garden gate as they make small talk about their journey.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig and two German women friends chat over coffee in the kitchen about dresses and someone named Helga Palitsch.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Aniela prepares a schnapps tray in the dining room while the women continue discussing Helga Palitsch's husband and bruises on her arms.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN/HALLWAY HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Aniela carries the schnapps tray down the hallway as the women discover a diamond hidden in toothpaste.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK GARDEN PATIO/HALLWAY/KITCHEN, HÖSS VILLA -
scenes.summary: Aniela sets up the schnapps on the patio as fifty SS officers in groups arrive from the garden gate, while she collects Rudolf's boots and the women discuss furs.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf, Bischoff, and the engineers examine technical drawings at the coffee table as Sander explains a chamber design that can incinerate four to five hundred at once in seven hours.
scenes.turningPointNote: Explicit discussion of crematorium capacity reveals the industrial scale of murder

scenes.heading: INT. HALL, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Aniela knocks politely on the closed glass doors of the living room, leaves Rudolf's boots, and departs.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, HÖSS VILLA
scenes.summary: Hedwig and her friends part the net curtains to watch through the kitchen window as applause sounds outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf raises a schnapps glass as fifty SS officers applaud him, toasts his birthday, then descends to shake hands with officers who look like they've just arrived from hell.

scenes.heading: INT. BOYS BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - LATER
scenes.summary: Claus changes into his pint-sized SS uniform while Hans plays with toy soldiers on the floor, mumbling commands at them.

scenes.heading: EXT. REEDS, AUSCHWITZ REGION - LATER
scenes.summary: Rudolf and Claus ride horses along an embankment between vast fields of bullrushes, hearing distant yelling and identifying the call of a Eurasian bittern.

scenes.heading: EXT. REEDS, AUSCHWITZ REGION - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: A mounted SS officer emerges from marsh reeds onto an embankment scattered with discarded apples, scanning the area before bursting back into the marsh.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM, HÖSS VILLA - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Rudolf sits at the dining room table charmed by birthday candles as the family gathers around, makes a wish, blows out candles, and they sing for he's a jolly good fellow.

scenes.heading: INT. BOYS’ BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Claus and Hans lie in their dark bunk beds with pocket torches as Claus inspects thirteen gold teeth, holding three up to the light while others lie on his bedsheet.
scenes.turningPointNote: Child playing with gold teeth from victims reveals how deeply the family is integrated into the machinery of murder

scenes.heading: EXT. LOOKING AT HÖSS VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rudolf walks up the porch steps back into the house as interior lights illuminate the downstairs and two upstairs windows.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, HÖSS VILLA - SAME
scenes.summary: Rudolf locks the back door and kitchen windows while turning off lights, with the family dog Dilla present.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf locks the glass doors to the dining room, draws curtains, and turns off the light.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL, HÖSS VILLA - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Rudolf secures the front door locks and turns off the hall light as the house becomes quiet.

scenes.heading: INT. LANDING, HÖSS VILLA - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Rudolf walks upstairs and turns off the landing light, revealing red-haloed smoke in the darkness before finding Inge-Brigit sitting trance-like by the window saying she's passing out sugars.
scenes.turningPointNote: Child's disturbed behavior and visible smoke from crematoriums create direct connection between family life and ongoing murder

scenes.heading: EXT. DITCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A young Polish girl uses thermal night vision to press apples into the earth wall of a ditch, creating a trail of partially buried apples every few yards.

scenes.heading: INT. GIRLS' BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA -CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf reads Hansel and Gretel to Inge-Brigit and Heideraud in their bedroom as flickering red light from outside dapples the wall and both girls fall asleep.

scenes.heading: EXT. DITCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Polish girl reaches the end of the ditch with an empty sack, having left a trail of buried apples, then climbs out into the pitch black night.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM , HÖSS VILLA - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Rudolf and Hedwig lie in separate beds discussing a phone call from Bracht and Hedwig's desire to return to an Italian spa, with Rudolf agreeing and cuddling up to her.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAILWAY SPUR, AUSCHWITZ STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Rudolf sits mounted on his horse looking impassively skyward as off-screen sounds reveal women, children and babies screaming in panic during a train arrival and selection process.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rudolf's calm oversight of mass arrival and selection process shows his direct involvement in systematic murder

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT OF HÖSS VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: The Höss children dressed for school emerge from the front door with satchels as Bracht's voice reads a letter praising Rudolf's unprecedented achievements at Auschwitz.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, HÖSS VILLA - DAY (CONTINUOUS)
scenes.summary: Rudolf methodically counts and sorts bills of different currencies into separate piles at his desk while Bracht's voice continues praising his unique accomplishments.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOLA RIVER - MORNING
scenes.summary: Rudolf paddles his canoe down the Sola River with Hans and Inge-Brigit, teaching them about storks flying to Africa as they drift peacefully downstream.

scenes.heading: EXT. HALL, HÖSS VILLA, BACK GARDEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Hedwig gardens in the flowerbeds with Claus and Heideraud, showing Claus how to distinguish weeds from flowers.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROCKY INLET, SOLA RIVER - MORNING
scenes.summary: Rudolf stands waist-deep fishing in the river while the children play in the shallows, but pulls out a human eye socket and bone fragment from the water.
scenes.turningPointNote: Discovery of human remains in the river reveals how the evidence of mass murder permeates even their recreational activities

scenes.heading: EXT. SOLA RIVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Rudolf paddles hard through choppy water as the weather has changed and the shivering children make sounds through their chattering teeth.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM, HÖSS VILLA - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Hedwig scrubs Hans and Inge-Brigit in the bathtub with soap and brushes as they protest, while Elfryda sings to distract them.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, HÖSS VILLA - SAME
scenes.summary: Rudolf lies distracted on the sofa reading when the telephone rings, and after checking his watch, he instructs the caller to hang up then calls Fritz back to ask about something.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rudolf receives what appears to be significant news requiring a secure callback

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, HÖSS VILLA - DAY (SHORT WHILE LATER)
scenes.summary: Hedwig's mother Linna arrives hot and sweaty from her train journey, discussing the trip delays and meeting other passengers moving to work at new factories.

scenes.heading: INT. GIRLS BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig shows Linna to the girls' bedroom where she'll be staying, and Linna approves of the lovely room and fresh flowers before they sit together on the bed.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM, HÖSS VILLA - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Hedwig and Linna watch Sophie, Marta, Elfryda and Aniela prepare party sandwiches and cakes, with Linna sampling cinnamon rolls made from her recipe.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, HÖSS VILLA - SAME
scenes.summary: Rudolf sits at his desk dictating a memo about SS members improperly picking from the lilac tree, demanding they cut flowers sensitively to decorate the entire camp.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig shows her mother Linna the back garden where the children are playing, explaining that Jews work in the house but live on the other side of the wall.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOP GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig gives Linna a tour of the upper garden with pool and gazebo, proudly showing off her design work while casually discussing the camp wall and making antisemitic remarks about former Jewish employers.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - DAY (CONTINUOUS)
scenes.summary: A montage of beautiful flowers in bloom is interrupted by increasingly loud sounds of torture from over the wall, as the screen gradually turns red.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA
scenes.summary: The Höss children and friends gather around a man showing them a stork as garden party entertainment.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, HÖSS VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Two wet schoolgirls read entries in the Höss house guestbook, finding praise for their National Socialist hospitality.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Rudolf paces agitatedly on the patio smoking while party sounds continue, then walks down as Claus courts his girlfriend nearby.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOP GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf watches children playing in the pool area while parents chat under the gazebo and Hedwig and Linna relax in loungers.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig follows Rudolf through the kitchen suggesting he call Bormann about his situation, and he responds that he's tried everything short of going to Hitler.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf leaves through the front door as Hedwig shouts after him, then she slips on water and angrily yanks Marta's hair while ordering her to clean it up.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Hedwig walks purposefully from the villa down to the river to find Rudolf.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER BANK, AUSCHWITZ - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf and Hedwig stand by the river discussing his impending transfer, with Hedwig pressing him about the reasons and timeline while he remains vague about the politics involved.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rudolf's transfer is confirmed, threatening the family's privileged life at Auschwitz

scenes.heading: EXT. TOP GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - DUSK
scenes.summary: Linna wakes alone after the swimming party to find a tall arc of black smoke drifting across the sky above the wall, which unnerves her.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM WINDOW, HÖSS VILLA - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Höss family eats turtle soup as Rudolf explains he'll miss Hans's birthday and their anniversary due to his transfer, calling their sacrifices worthwhile.

scenes.heading: INT. GIRL’S BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - NIGHT, LATER.
scenes.summary: Linna lies awake in bed while red light flickers on the walls and a baby cries upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. ELFRYDA’S ATTIC ROOM, HÖSS VILLA, CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Elfryda sits drunk on her bed pouring vodka while baby Annagret screams in her cot.

scenes.heading: EXT. WINDOW, GIRLS BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA, CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Linna looks through the window at distant red crematorium flames reflected in the glass, then quickly drops the curtain.

scenes.heading: INT. GIRL’S BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA, CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Linna holds a handkerchief to her nose while Hans plays with shadow shapes from his torch in the boys' room.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA, CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig sleeps peacefully in bed, undisturbed by hellish sounds from the camp.

scenes.heading: INT. STABLES AUSCHWITZ - NIGHT, LATER
scenes.summary: Rudolf feeds carrots to his horse Merer in the stables, talking tenderly to her about how difficult the transfer will be for both of them.

scenes.heading: INT. RUDOLF’S OFFICE , COMMANDANT BUILDING AUSCHWITZ - NIGHT,
scenes.summary: Rudolf dictates letters from his office, including one about a ring crematorium and another requesting his family be allowed to stay at their address during his transfer.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT TUNNEL - NIGHT, L ATER
scenes.summary: Rudolf walks through an underground tunnel from the camp while his voice continues dictating a letter about allowing his family to remain.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT, HÖSS VILLA, CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf emerges from the tunnel into his villa's basement and washes at the laundry sink.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig sleeps alone in the master bedroom next to Rudolf's still-made empty bed.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf adjusts his appearance in the basement mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL, HÖSS VILLA - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Rudolf emerges from the basement and finds Inge-Brigit sleepwalking in the pantry.

scenes.heading: INT. PANTRY, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf gently picks up sleepwalking Inge-Brigit, who comments that he's sweaty.

scenes.heading: EXT. ORCHARD, BRZESZCZE - NIGHT (CONTINUOUS)
scenes.summary: In thermal night vision, Polish girl Aleksandra gathers pears from trees while Rudolf's voice reads Hansel and Gretel off-screen.
scenes.turningPointNote: First glimpse of resistance activity outside the villa's bubble of normalcy

scenes.heading: EXT. ORCHARD/CONSTRUCTION SITE, BRZESZCZE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Aleksandra carries a sack of pears past SS warning signs while Rudolf's voice continues reading the fairy tale about the witch being pushed into the oven.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONSTRUCTION SITE, BRZESZCZE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Aleksandra walks across a construction site where thousands of prisoners work during the day, as Rudolf's voice finishes the story about the witch being cooked alive.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf undresses for bed while Hedwig sleeps in the other bed.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONSTRUCTION SITE, BRZESZCZE - CONTINUO US
scenes.summary: In thermal vision, Aleksandra deposits pears near spades in the ground and discovers a small tin can with a folded paper inside, replacing it with a pear.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRY ROADSIDE, BRZESZCZE - LATER
scenes.summary: Aleksandra hides on her bicycle among trees, waiting for SS men and a pig to pass before crossing the street.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK-STREETS, BRZESZCZE OUTSKIRTS - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Aleksandra freewheels down a track toward houses and pushes her bicycle through a back yard.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALEKSANDRA’S STREET, BRZESZCZE OUTSKIRTS - SHORT WHILE
scenes.summary: Aleksandra parks her bicycle and hurries into her apartment building as thermal night vision ends.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY, ALEKSANDRA’S APARTMENT BUILDING, BRZESZCZE -
scenes.summary: Aleksandra's mother Wanda opens the door for her daughter's return.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM. ALEKSANDRA’S APARTMENT, BRZESZCZE - LATER
scenes.summary: Wanda embroiders by kerosene light while Aleksandra sleeps, then a foul smell causes her to close windows overlooking blazing crematorium chimneys.

scenes.heading: INT. ALEKSANDRA’S BEDROOM, APARTMENT, BRZESZCZE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wanda closes Aleksandra's bedroom window against the view of crematorium flames while an archive recording of Joseph Wulf speaking in Yiddish begins.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, ALEKSANDRA’S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wanda carries a kerosene light upstairs as the Yiddish voice continues speaking.

scenes.heading: INT. 2ND FLOOR LANDING, ALEKSANDRA’S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wanda opens a door to the balcony.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALCONY, ALEKSANDRA’S APARTMENT, BRZESZCZE - SAME
scenes.summary: In thermal vision, Wanda rescues washing from swirling ash on the balcony while the Yiddish poem continues about imprisoned hearts that are not yet cold.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, OLUSIA’S APARTMENT, O Ś WI Ę CIM - DAWN
scenes.summary: Aleksandra plays piano, sight-reading prisoner-composed music from the paper she found buried in the tin can.
scenes.turningPointNote: The resistance message is revealed as music, showing organized prisoner communication and cultural resistance

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, HÖSS VILLA - MORNING
scenes.summary: The four eldest Höss children dress for school while Elfryda hooks their satchels onto their raised arms in a daily Nazi salute practice.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM, HÖSS VILLA - MORNING
scenes.summary: Hedwig sits alone at the dining table with breakfast for two, then gets up to call for someone upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sophie tells Hedwig that her mother is gone and her case is missing, prompting Hedwig to rush upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. GIRLS' BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig confirms that Linna's case is gone and the bed has been made.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig asks Sophie about a note from her mother, frustrated when Sophie hasn't looked for one.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig finds a folded note addressed to her in the guest book, reads it, then looks outside before returning to the dining room.
scenes.turningPointNote: Linna's departure note reveals her rejection of the family's situation

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM, HÖSS VILLA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hedwig burns Linna's note and threatens Aniela, saying her husband could spread the maid's ashes across fields.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM, HÖSS VILLA - LATER
scenes.summary: Hedwig lies stretched out in the bathtub.

scenes.heading: EXT. OPEN COUNTRY, AUSCHWITZ REGION - DAY
scenes.summary: Rudolf and Claus ride horses through open countryside as Rudolf explains he'll be head of all commandants, inspecting camps and their yield.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - DAY, LATER
scenes.summary: Elfryda, Heideraud and Inge-Brigit sit at a patio table pressing flowers, with Elfryda explaining that one species is called "girl's eye" because its petals resemble women's eyelashes.

scenes.heading: EXT. GREENHOUSE, TOP GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Kapo Böhner repairs the greenhouse roof with wooden planks under Hedwig's supervision as she walks into the greenhouse.

scenes.heading: INT. GREENHOUSE, TOP GARDEN, HOSS VILLA - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Hedwig smokes in the greenhouse and offers a cigarette to Böhner, then watches him smoke while he watches her.

scenes.heading: INT. BOYS' BEDROOM, HÖSS VILLA - LATER
scenes.summary: Hans plays with dice and soldiers in his bedroom when he hears Rudolf outside shouting orders to drown a prisoner who was fighting over an apple, causing Hans to look out the window then quickly pull back.

scenes.heading: EXT. BANDSTAND, TOWN PARK, ORANIENBURG - MORNING, WINTER
scenes.summary: Rudolf walks through a snowy park in Oranienburg and stops to listen to a German military brass band playing at the bandstand.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, CONCENTRATION CAMPS INSPECTORATE (CCI),
scenes.summary: Concentration camp commandants approach through a corporate office corridor while SS officers stand by open doors and a tannoy announcement continues.

scenes.heading: INT. BOARDROOM, CCI, ORANIENBURG - DAY, CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: An SS waiter wheels out an empty trolley while Rudolf's adjutant places file folders at chairs around a boardroom table as another adjutant reads names to Rudolf.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, CCI, ORANIENBURG - DAY, CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Camp commandants and their adjutants gather in the corridor chatting while waiting for the meeting to begin as names continue to be read over the intercom.

scenes.heading: INT. BOARDROOM, CCI, ORANIENBURG - DAY, SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Pohl addresses the assembled camp commandants about the Führer's approval to deport and exterminate Hungary's 700,000 Jews for extermination and war production.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major escalation in genocide operations is officially announced

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN PARK, ORANIENBURG - DUSK
scenes.summary: Rudolf walks home from work through the snowy park carrying his briefcase alongside other SS officers.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM, RUDOLF’S LODGING, ORANIENBURG - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rudolf sleeps in his bed in pajamas with work papers scattered on the floor and a small heater running.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN PARK, ORANIENBURG - DAY, MORNING
scenes.summary: Rudolf walks to work through the park past the empty bandstand with other SS staff and nurses, with black smoke visible in the background from a Berlin bombing raid.

scenes.heading: INT. RUDOLF'S OFFICE, CCI ORANIENBURG, NEAR BERLIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Rudolf stands at his office window in a reverie, gazing out while typewriters clack behind him.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOP GARDEN, HÖSS VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Claus and Hans play in the winter garden where Claus locks Hans in the greenhouse and sits on the wall making a gas hissing sound while the crematorium chimney belches black smoke.

scenes.heading: INT. WAITING ROOM, OUTSIDE GLÜCK’S OFFICE, CCI, ORANIENBURG -
scenes.summary: Rudolf stands with Wilhelm Burger in a waiting room outside Glück's office while a tannoy announcement plays about a funeral for bombing victims.

scenes.heading: INT. RICHARD GLÜCKS OFFICE, CCI, ORANIENBURG - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Glücks hands Rudolf a telegram stating that his name came up in discussions with Himmler about moving Liebehenschel out of Auschwitz and reassigning Rudolf there to handle the Hungarian operation.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rudolf receives orders to return to Auschwitz for the Hungarian deportations

scenes.heading: INT. SS SURGERY, ORANIENBURG - DAY
scenes.summary: Rudolf undergoes a medical examination in his underpants while an SS doctor checks him with a stethoscope and asks about his sleep and bodily functions.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR NEAR SS SURGERY, CCI, ORANIENBURG - LATER
scenes.summary: Rudolf makes a long-distance phone call to Hedwig from a corridor telephone, excitedly telling her he has news about rumors of his reassignment.

scenes.heading: EXT. COURTYARD, POHL RESIDENCE, BERLIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rudolf stands alone smoking at a party in Pohl's courtyard by tables with champagne and an ice sculpture swastika while Hedwig's voice continues over the scene about looking in a compact.

scenes.heading: INT. ANTLER ROOM, POHL RESIDENCE, BERLIN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf enters a room covered with antler trophies where party guests are arriving and watching a string quartet.

scenes.heading: INT. SALON, POHL RESIDENCE, BERLIN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf awkwardly enters a salon full of Nazi dignitaries, SS officers, and civilians in formal attire and greets Eleanor Pohl who seems animated and happy to see him.

scenes.heading: INT. PIANO ROOM, POHL RESIDENCE, BERLIN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Rudolf is greeted by Dr. Meindl and a group of senior SS officers including Eichmann, with an older SS man congratulating him and Eichmann toasting him with champagne.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAND HALLWAY, POHL RESIDENCE - SHORT WHILE LATER
scenes.summary: Rudolf makes his way through the grand hallway to observe the ballroom, then climbs stairs to find a better vantage point on a darker landing.

scenes.heading: INT. BALCONY, POHL RESIDENCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf walks onto a balcony and peers down at the ballroom below, surveying the throng of guests with his lips moving as if calculating.

scenes.heading: INT. RUDOLF'S OFFICE, HÖSS VILLA - NIGHT (CONTINUOUS)
scenes.summary: Hedwig stands by Rudolf's desk holding the phone in her nightdress, having been called from bed, telling him it's the middle of the night.

scenes.heading: INT. RUDOLF’S OFFICE, CCI, ORANIENBURG - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rudolf sits at his office desk with feet up on the windowsill, speaking to Hedwig on the phone while watching snow fall outside, apologizing for calling so late.

scenes.heading: INT. RUDOLF'S OFFICE, HÖSS VILLA - NIGHT (CONTINUOUS)
scenes.summary: Hedwig tells Rudolf he can tell her about it when he's back.

scenes.heading: INT. RUDOLF’S OFFI CE, CCI, ORANIENBURG - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rudolf asks if Hedwig has told the children that Daddy's coming home.

scenes.heading: INT. RUDOLF'S OFFICE, HÖSS VILLA - NIGHT (CONTINUOUS)
scenes.summary: Hedwig says yes, says goodnight, and puts down the phone before starting to walk away.

scenes.heading: INT. RUDOLF’S OFFICE, CCI, ORANIENBURG - NIGHT (CONTINUOUS)
scenes.summary: Rudolf hangs up the phone and walks out of his office.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY, HÖSS VILLA - NIGHT (CONTINUOUS)
scenes.summary: Hedwig enters the hallway and turns off the light on her way to bed.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR/STAIRS, CCI , ORANIENBURG - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf locks his office door and walks down the corridor pumping his fists in self-congratulation with a growing smile and skip in his step, then suddenly clutches the bannister and vomits on the floor.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rudolf's physical reaction reveals the psychological cost of his excitement about returning to genocide

scenes.heading: INT. LOWER CORRIDOR, CCI, ORANIENBURG - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf convulses again with a final long wretch and spit, then cautiously straightens up holding his hat and looks down the empty dark corridor.

scenes.heading: INT. BLACK SPACE
scenes.summary: A door opens from inside a dark space as two Polish women cleaners in present-day blue outfits enter carrying cleaning materials, revealing dark cement walls and modern signage.
scenes.turningPointNote: Film shifts to present-day museum cleaning, bridging past and present

scenes.heading: EXT. CREMATORIUM 1, AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU STATE MUSEUM - DAWN,
scenes.summary: The cleaners bring their materials to Crematorium 1 at the Auschwitz-Birkenau State Museum, the same chimney previously seen from the Höss garden.

scenes.heading: INT. GAS CHAMBER, CREMATORIUM 1, AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU STATE
scenes.summary: One cleaner sweeps the concrete floor of the gas chamber in Crematorium 1.

scenes.heading: INT. CREMATORIUM 1, AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU STATE MUSEUM - DAWN,
scenes.summary: Another cleaner wipes the black cast iron corpse delivery trolleys and brick furnaces built by Topf and Sons.

scenes.heading: INT. EXHIBIT ROOM, BLOCK 5, AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU STATE MUSEUM -
scenes.summary: A cleaner carries a vacuum past a wall of 1930s suitcases with family names and addresses while another cleaner wipes display glass protecting piles of children's shoes.

scenes.heading: INT. EXH IBIT CORRIDOR BLOCK 5, AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU STATE
scenes.summary: Two cleaners wipe glass protecting vast piles of shoes on either side of a corridor while another cleaner vacuums the floor beneath the floor-to-ceiling mountain of shoes.

scenes.heading: INT. EXHIBIT ROOM BLOCK 5, AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU STATE MUSEUM -
scenes.summary: A cleaner vacuums in front of a display case of 1930s/1940s prosthetic limbs, crutches and supports.

scenes.heading: INT. EXHIBIT ROOM BLOCK 6, AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU STATE MUSEUM -
scenes.summary: A cleaner wipes the glass of a display case containing blue and white striped prisoner uniforms.

scenes.heading: INT. EXHIBIT CORRIDOR BLOCK 6, AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU STATE
scenes.summary: A cleaner vacuums a long corridor lined with black and white framed photographs while two other cleaners wipe the photographs of shaved-headed Auschwitz prisoner registrations.

scenes.heading: INT. LOWER CORRIDOR, CCI, ORANIENBURG - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rudolf stands in the same position looking down the CCI corridor exactly as when he finished vomiting, holding his officer's cap by his side in silence.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL. CCI, ORANIENBURG - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rudolf descends the staircase with increasing confidence, puts his cap on his head, and continues down multiple floors until his footsteps fade away as alarming end music begins.
scenes.turningPointNote: Film's final moments as Rudolf descends into darkness with ominous music, completing his return to genocidal duty
